ask: Raw log pull with no filter → tens of KB into context. Re-run piped, e.g. `… | grep -C 50 -iE 'error|fail|fatal'` or `| tail -n 200`.
Agent/Task prompted for exhaustive prose → big hidden output-token bill. Rewrite the prompt to request: bulleted file paths + components only, no narrative, cap ~150 lines.

Spawning a subagent for light exploration = fresh session = one expensive cache WRITE that gets 0 subsequent reads. Cache writes cost ~3× reads; a write amortizes only after ~25 re-reads in the same session. Fix: use Bash/Read/grep in-thread instead. Long-lived single sessions hit cache hundreds of times for the same prefix cost.

Reading a large file (>20 KB) without an offset dumps the whole thing into context → busts the cache prefix, forcing an expensive full rewrite. Use `offset`+`limit` to read only the relevant block, or use Bash `grep -n` to locate the target lines first.

Agent returning a long report directly into main thread → large tool result injects into context mid-session, busts cache prefix. Instruct the agent to write detailed findings to a temp file (e.g. /tmp/findings.md) and return only a 2-3 sentence summary. Cache write cost exceeding read benefit is caused by exactly this pattern.
Large Write over an existing file regenerates the whole thing in output tokens. Prefer targeted Edit(s) for the changed regions. Use Write only for new files or full rewrites.

note: Trivial command on current model. Consider /model sonnet for routine work; escalate when stuck.
